etymology.description: е‘·(е‘·) (xiДЃ, "sip") + е“є(е“є) (bЗ”, "nourish") - combining to convey the meaning of 'sipping to nourish' as associated with consuming liquids or soft foods.
etymology.interpretation: The character е‘·(е‘·) (xiДЃ, "sip") evokes the action of taking small amounts of liquid or food into the mouth, which is commonly done to savor or ease the digestion process. е“є(е“є) (bЗ”, "nourish") involves feeding or providing sustenance, typically through gentle or care-filled means. Together, they construct an image of nurturing oneself or others by sipping, highlighting the delicate manner in which nourishment is obtained.
